question: Identify the life cycle methods of JSP.
Which of the following activity is involved in Sprint Review?

options.label: A
options.text: Inspect and seek feedback from the stakeholders.

options.label: B
options.text: Seek approval for the work done.

answer: Inspect and seek feedback from the stakeholders.

question: Which of the following are important responsibilities of a Scrum Master? Select Three.

options.label: B
options.text: Bridging development team & Product owner to create high-value products

options.label: C
options.text: Facilitating scrum events as requested or needed

options.label: D
options.text: Ordering the items in the Product Backlog

options.label: E
options.text: Changing the Product Backlog item's priority

question: Which artifact is used to list user stories to be completed in each iteration? Choose the most appropriate option.

Which of the following statements are INCORRECT about scrum master?
Scrum master facilitates the scrum team and the product owner to perform day to day activities.
Scrum master Leads the discussion in Scrum Events or Meetings.
Scrum master is Customer who has given the requirements.

Which of the following statements are CORRECT?
$(i)$ Scrum Team is a group of 7-10 members with all the skills to develop, test and deliver the working product.
$(ii)$ Product Owner is the customer who has given the requirements.

What will be the output of the code given?
public class Test {
public static void main(String[] args) {
int accounts[] = {101, 1012, 183};
System.out.print("A");
int accId = accounts[3]; // Line-X
System.out.print("B");
}
}

options.label: C
options.text: Compilation Error at Line-X: Unhandled Exception type ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException

question:
What will be the output of the code given below?
public class Test {
public static void main(String[] args) {
int accounts[] = {101, 1012, 103};
try {
System.out.print("A");
int accId = accounts[3];
System.out.print("B");
} catch(ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException exception) {
System.out.print("E1");
} catch(Exception exception) {
System.out.print("E2");
}
}
}

What would be the output of the code given below?
public class Test {
public static void main(String[] args) {
try {
test(8);
} catch(Exception exception) {
System.out.println("exception-Main");
}
}
public static void test(int data) {
if (data > 0) {
throw new Exception(); // Line-X
}
System.out.println("End Test");
}
}

options.label: A
options.text: Compilation Error at Line-X: Unhandled exception type Exception

answer: Compilation Error at Line-X: Unhandled exception type Exception

question:
What would be the output of the code given below?
public class Demo {
public static void main(String[] args) {
try {
test();
} catch(Exception exception) {
System.out.println("Main Catch");
}
}
public static void test() { // Line-X
try {
// ...
} finally {
throw new RuntimeException(); // Line-Y
System.out.println("Test Finally"); // Unreachable
}
}
}

options.label: C
options.text: Compilation Error at Line-Y: Unreachable statement

options.label: D
options.text: Compilation Error at Line-X: missing exception propagation type ArithmeticException

answer: Compilation Error at Line-Y: Unreachable statement

question:
Identify the CORRECT statement with respect to List and Set.
$(i)$ The data inserted in List will be in sorted order always
$(ii)$ Set allows to insert duplicate values

What will be the output of the code given below? Assume that all required classes and interfaces are imported.
Map myMap = new TreeMap();
myMap.put(16, 150);
myMap.put(30, 25);
myMap.put(20, 50);
Set keySet = myMap.keySet();
for (Integer key: keySet) {
System.out.print(myMap.get(key) + " ");
}

What will be the output of the code given? Assume that all required classes and interfaces are imported.
Set mySet = new TreeSet();
mySet.add(10);
mySet.add(3);
mySet.add(20);
for (Integer data: mySet) {
mySet.add(3);
System.out.print(data + " ");
}

$(i)$ Create a class by extending Thread class and override run() method
$(ii)$ Create a class by implementing Runnable interface and defining start() method in it

Which of the following is/are the CORRECT declaration of Functional interface?
$(i)$
interface TestInterface {
public void add(int num1, int num2);
public default String display(String name) { return null; }
}
$(ii)$
@FunctionalInterface
interface TestInterface {
public void add(int num1, int num2);
public static String display(String name) { return null; }
}

Which of the following code can be placed at Line-X so that the code displays the output as "Java"? [Choose 2]
interface TestInterface {
void display(String message);
}
public class Demo1 {
public static void main(String[] args) {
// Line-X
obj.display("Java");
}
}

options.label: C
options.text: TestInterface obj = new TestInterface(String message) -> System.out.println(message);

What will happen in the customer table, if the below JPA code executes successfully?
CustomerEntity entity = new CustomerEntity();
entity.setCustomerId(1001);
entity.setCustomerName("Justin");
entityManager.getTransaction().begin();
entityManager.merge(entity); // Line X
entityManager.getTransaction().commit();

options.label: A
options.text: Deletes record with customerId "1001" and inserts a record with customerName "Justin"

options.label: B
options.text: Just updates customerName to "Justin" whose customerId is 1001

options.label: C
options.text: Runtime Error at Line X: Duplicate entry for id

options.label: D
options.text: Inserts a new record with customerId "1001" and customerName "Justin"

answer: Just updates customerName to "Justin" whose customerId is 1001

Choose the valid implementation of the delete query to delete customers who have credit less than a particular value.

options.label: A
options.text:
Option 1:
@Modifying
@Query("delete from CustomerEntity k where k.credit < :cpoint")
int delete(@Param("cpoint") double credit);

options.label: B
options.text:
Option 2:
@Query("delete from CustomerEntity k where k.credit > :cpoint")
int delete(@Param("cpoint") double credit);

options.label: C
options.text:
Option 3:
@Query("delete from CustomerEntity k where k.credit > :cpoint")
int delete(double cpoint);

options.label: D
options.text:
Option 4:
@Modifying
@Query("delete from CustomerEntity k")
int delete(@Param("cpoint") double credit);

answer:
Option 1:
@Modifying
@Query("delete from CustomerEntity k where k.credit < :cpoint")
int delete(@Param("cpoint") double credit);

question:
Which of the following is/are CORRECT with respect to Spring transaction methods?
$(i)$ If a method throws runtime/unchecked exception, then rollback will be triggered implicitly
$(ii)$ If a method throws checked exception, then rollback will be triggered implicitly

Which of the following is CORRECT with respect to Spring transaction propagation types?
$(i)$ propagation type Propagation.REQUIRED starts a new transaction, if a method is invoked from another method and a transaction does not exist.
$(ii)$ propagation type Propagation.REQUIRES_NEW starts a new transaction, if a method is invoked from another method and a transaction already exists.

Refer to the Functional interface MyInterface { void test(int a, int b); }. Choose from below a valid Lambda Implementation. [Choose 2] (Assume Java 11)

options.label: B
options.text: MyInterface my1 = (var a, var b) -> System.out.println("Result " + (a+b));

options.label: C
options.text: var my1 = (a, b) -> System.out.println("Result " + (a+b)); (Invalid compilation syntax without explicit assignment LHS interface context target)

options.label: D
options.text: var my1 = (int a, int b) -> System.out.println("Result " + (a+b));
